paragraphs.text: KPMG WORKPLAN
The scope of work proposed by KPMG is outlined below and can be divided into the following workstreams:
Financial due diligence
Other business analysis (optional)
Canadian tax due diligence
Tax advisory services
A. Financial Due Diligence

Our work will focus on the analysis of historical financial results and balance sheet profile of Target for the fiscal years ended March 31, 2022 (“FY22”), 2023 (“FY23”), 2024 (“FY24”) and most recent trailing twelve-month period ended (“TTM Jun-24”) or latest information made available.
Overview
Obtain background information of Target, including its:
Organizational structure, ownership and management reporting relationships;
Range of products, customer segments and regions served; and
Financial reporting and information systems (including process, timing, structure of financial reporting, and interaction with the information/accounting systems).
Consistency of accounting policies
Read Target’s financial statements and discuss them with Target’s management to gain an understanding of accounting policies and practices including:
Reporting methodology;
Revenue recognition policies;
Frequency of standard cost updates and procedures for analyzing variances;
Significant accounting estimates (e.g. bad debt provision, rebates, capitalized R&D, etc.); and
Recent or contemplated changes in accounting principles.
Sell-side Quality of Earnings assessment

Based on the analysis and discussion with the sell-side due diligence advisor PwC, comment on Management’s proposed adjustments to reported EBITDA and inquire about the nature and rationale of such adjustments and supporting documentation;
Perform a traffic light assessment of Management and sell-side proposed adjustments; and
Propose any significant adjustments identified that are not reflected in the sell-side Quality of Earnings analysis.
Quality of earnings
Identify potential adjustments to reported earnings before interest, income taxes, depreciation, and amortization (“EBITDA”) by considering the impact of:
Management’s proposed adjustments to reported EBITDA (e.g. out-of period inventory adjustments, non-recurring professional fees, Dot customer normalizations etc.);
The impact of acquisitions (e.g. Tasty Selections Inc);
The impact of supply chain disruptions, if any;
The timing of true-ups and other adjustments made less frequently than monthly;
The impact of any changes to senior management or team structure;
The impacts of foreign exchange including constant currency analysis;
The impact of commodity / raw material cost fluctuations and any associated hedging programs;
The impact of any key supplier cost arrangements including assessment of hedged vs. market prices and illustrative impacts to EBITDA;
The impact of Le Bon lease renewal;
The impact of related party transactions (i.e. non-fair market pricing, personal expenses, donations etc.); and
Other potential items discovered in the due diligence process.
Supporting analyses to quality/sustainability of earnings
Revenue and margin analysis (to the extent available)
Obtain and read an analysis of Target’s revenue and inquire about:
Gross to net revenue;
Revenue and gross margin by plant;
Revenue and gross margin by brand (Redstone, Le Bon, Tasty Selection etc.);
Revenue and gross margin by product category (bread, frozen dough rolls, garlic toast, cookies, muffins etc.);
Revenue and gross margin by customer;
Revenue and gross margin by SKU;
Organic growth and delineation between price vs. volume;
Pricing practices;
Promotions and discounts;
Any non-recurring components of revenue;
Revenue seasonality, if any;
Raw material costs including hedging programs and high level evaluation of embedded inventory gains and/or losses; and
Other components of cost of sales and key suppliers.
Refer to other business analysis section for in-depth revenue and margin analysis.
Expenses
Obtain and read an analysis of Target’s operating expenses and inquire about:
High-level overview of employee compensation and benefits including:
Headcount and average compensation costs;
Accrued employee obligations and other provisions;
Severance / commitments, if any;
Incentive based compensation;
Flag any material gaps in headcount; and
Reconcile employee census to the financial statements.
Rent and logistics;
Food safety and quality costs;
Research and development costs;
Selling expenses;
Fixed vs. variable expense analysis; and
Other operating and G&A expenses.
Working capital
Summarize and normalize historical working capital in the business including:
Commenting on the working capital trends, for the respective period;
Understanding key drivers (i.e., seasonality, accounts payable days, days sales outstanding, days inventory outstanding); and
Identifying large or unusual non-recurring items that may have impacted normal working capital trends including potential items identified within the quality of earnings analysis noted above.
Supporting analyses to net working capital
Accounts receivable
Obtain and read an analysis of Target’s accounts receivable and inquire about:
Aging analysis and related credit terms (including special terms and changes thereto e.g. early payment discount elimination);
Trade and non-trade balances;
Allowance for uncollectible accounts and write-offs; and
Reserve and adjustments.
Inventory
Obtain and read an analysis of Target’s inventory and inquire about:
Composition by product category;
Inventory turnover by category;
Aging, slow-moving, excess and obsolete inventory;
Impairment and reserve calculations;
Consistency of accounting practices related to inventory valuation;
Purchasing procedures; and
Vendor rebates and allowances.
Accounts payable and accrued liabilities
Obtain and read an analysis of Target’s accounts payable and accrued liabilities and inquire about:
AP aging analysis and days outstanding;
Terms of trade with major vendors;
Payroll related funding and timing of cash flows;
Cadence and consistency of bonus and other incentive compensation; and
Other current and non-current liabilities.
Prepaids
Obtain and read an analysis of Target’s prepaid expenses and inquire about:
Composition of balances; and
Understand timing of cash flows vs. waterfall / timing of expense recognition.
Net debt
Summarize and comment on net debt items (on and off balance sheet) presented by management and other potential debt-like items; and
Consider whether elements of working capital have the nature of and may be reclassified as net debt, and if so, summarize the potential impact of these adjustments on working capital and net debt.
Capital expenditure requirements
Obtain and read an analysis of Target’s existing capital costs and inquire about:
Historical growth vs. maintenance capex;
If any deferred and planned capital expenditures; and
Impairment write-downs and issues.
High-level assessment of ASPE to IFRS / US GAAP

Review financial information and financial statements to gain an understanding of Target’s key accounting policies and practices. Summarize any potential adjustments between ASPE and IFRS/US GAAP that may impact historical EBITDA or balance sheet accounts.
Related party transactions
Inquire about and summarize related party transactions including:
Nature and extent of related party transactions; and
Basis of pricing for arrangements with related parties.
Commitments and contingencies
Inquire about significant commitments and contingent liabilities including:
Pending or threatened litigations by regulatory or other authorities and by employees;
Incentive compensation and employee benefit obligations; and
Significant lease and purchase obligations.
B. Other Business Analysis (Optional)
In-depth revenue and gross margin sustainability assessment
Leverage transactional level data to understand historical and forecasted drivers of revenue and margin across key dimensions of the business including:
By plant;
By brand (Redstone, Le Bon, Tasty Selection etc.);
By product category and subcategories (bread, frozen dough rolls, garlic toast, cookies, muffins etc.);
By country (US, Canada, ROW);
By customer and customer type;
Other KPIs as made available by Target; and
Other derivations and combination of fields (based on discussions with you).
This will include understanding the sustainability of product categories and bridging how price, volume or cost drivers impact historical and forecasted profitability.
C. Canadian Tax Due Diligence

Our Canadian tax due diligence procedures will be performed with respect to the proposed acquisition of the “Canadian Target”. Our professional fees will be based on the actual time required to complete the agreed upon work at agreed hourly rates identified for the professionals assigned to the engagement. Neither the amount of our fees nor the payment of our fees and expenses will be contingent upon transaction completion.  On the assumption that there is only two Canadian entities, we estimate our fees for the agreed procedures and deliverables will be in the range of $40,000 to $50,000 for the tax due diligence procedures below.

Unless otherwise specifically noted below, our scope of work will be restricted to the open taxation years, presumably periods FY20 – FY23 and YTD FY24 (hereinafter termed as “period under investigation"). References to accounts and associated data are for these periods.

Due diligence procedures will be undertaken through discussions with management, and where applicable, Canadian Target’s tax advisors, and through reading limited documents as described below. The procedures we perform are modified to meet each client's informational requests. The procedures which are to be performed in this instance will be agreed with the client in advance, prior to commencing work.
General
Inquire with Canadian Target’s management and/or their tax advisors about;
Overview of Canadian and foreign business operations (including provinces in which Canadian Target carries on business/has a permanent establishment);
Significant transactions in the period under investigation (including strategies, acquisitions, dispositions, and reorganizations) and tax treatment thereof;
Tax planning profile/strategies implemented, and benefits obtained;

Results of tax audits in the period under investigation subject to audit, quantum of known or proposed audit adjustments, and status of current/ongoing audits (income tax, sales tax, and payroll tax);
Nature of systems to ensure compliance with tax (income, sales, transfer pricing) laws;
Potential limitations on the use of tax attributes;
Payments to non-residents, related party transactions and transfer pricing methods, if any; and
Indirect tax status of Canadian Target’s revenue (e.g., taxable/non-taxable supplies and recovery of input tax credits).

Obtain and read Canadian corporate tax returns for the period under investigation filed by the Canadian Target, legal entity financial statements, supporting work papers, and other relevant information, as made available to us.

Obtain and read correspondence with tax authorities in respect of completed, ongoing and pending tax audits, most current statements of account, and notices of assessment/reassessment, as made available to us.

Obtain and read schedules of tax basis of significant assets of Canadian Target and unused loss carry overs by jurisdiction (if any), including date of expiration and applicable restrictions on use. Consider implications of an acquisition of control of Canadian Target on the availability of unused losses, and tax basis of significant assets, as applicable.
Obtain and read any Canadian tax opinions and/or memorandums regarding open tax years.
Obtain and read a sample (e.g., 1 per month) of GST/HST/QST/PST returns and remittances for the past 6 months and supporting working papers.
Reporting
Based on the above sources of information, consider, and prepare a tax due diligence report on significant potential tax exposures identified.
D. Tax Advisory Services (Optional)

Where requested, we may also provide tax modelling, structuring and other tax advisory assistance. Such work is outside scope of tax due diligence and subject to additional fees and is based on the actual time incurred at agreed rates. In the context of this transaction, such service may include (but are not limited to):
Tax Structuring
Preparation of a detailed tax plan re: integration and combination of the Target entities in a tax efficient manner.
Preliminary investigation of feasible alternatives for acquiring, holding, and exiting from the investment in a tax effective manner.
Analysis on the tax implications of proposed transaction steps or pre-closing transaction steps, as required by you;
Further research and analysis of the tax implications of the chosen structure including consideration of:
Tax-efficient strategies to finance the transaction through debt / equity and push-down interest costs on the acquisition, considering:
the application of the new excessive interest and financing expense limitation (“EIFEL”) regime,
Tax deductibility of transaction costs, unamortized deferred financing fees, etc.; and
Tax treatment of employment compensation including stock compensation (if any).
Any other related tax advice or assistance with respect to the tax consequences of the proposed transaction or activities post-investment.
Reporting

Our deliverable will be presentation in a PowerPoint deck format of one or two recommended acquisition structures including high level Canadian tax commentary on intended tax benefits and key potential tax issues.
SPA Commentary

Read initial draft of the Share Purchase Agreement and offer commentary to Client and its legal counsel concerning sections relating to Canadian tax matters based on the results of the due diligence assistance we provided.  You agree to review with your attorney all our comments and suggestions concerning the draft agreements before acting on any of our suggestions.
Tax Modelling
Comment on the accuracy of tax assumptions adopted in the financial models for Canadian Target including:
Relevant prevailing corporate tax rates (and potential changes) and key permanent differences between accounting and taxable income that impact the historical effective tax rates;
Consideration of key tax attributes; and
The impact of deferred tax balances on annual tax expense.
